content:
## Purpose and Scope

The Core Information Standard provides a comprehensive framework for capturing and sharing essential person-centred information across health and social care settings. This standard defines the core data elements necessary to support integrated care delivery, ensuring that critical information about an individual's demographics, care needs, preferences, and circumstances is consistently recorded and accessible to all professionals involved in their care.

The standard encompasses fundamental information categories including person demographics, care preferences through the 'About Me' section, individual requirements for reasonable adjustments, alerts, legal information, safeguarding considerations, and comprehensive health and care records. It supports both clinical and social care contexts, promoting a holistic view of the person that extends beyond medical conditions to include their personal preferences, social circumstances, and individual needs.

## Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder consultation, the Core Information Standard addresses the longstanding challenge of fragmented information across health and social care systems. It recognises that effective care coordination requires not just clinical data, but also understanding of the person's individual circumstances, preferences, and support needs. The standard incorporates requirements from the Equality Act 2010 for reasonable adjustments and aligns with mental capacity and safeguarding legislation to ensure comprehensive capture of legal and protective information.
title: Introduction

content:
## Key Recommendations

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person-Centred Information Capture | Implement comprehensive recording of individual preferences, communication needs, and personal circumstances alongside clinical data | PRSB Standards | High |
| Reasonable Adjustments Recording | Systematically document all reasonable adjustments required under the Equality Act 2010, including communication support, specific information formats, and mobility needs | Equality Act 2010, NHS Accessible Information Standard | Mandatory |
| Safeguarding Documentation | Record safeguarding concerns, looked after child status, and child protection plans with appropriate professional oversight and review dates | Children Act 1989/2004, Care Act 2014 | Mandatory |
| Mental Capacity Assessment | Document mental capacity assessments, best interest decisions, and lasting power of attorney details when relevant | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | High |
| Care Plan Integration | Capture care and support plans, contingency plans, and end of life care preferences in structured format with clear review dates | NICE Quality Standards | High |
| Medication Management | Maintain comprehensive medication records including prescriptions, over-the-counter medications, discontinued items, and medication changes | NICE Medicines Management Guidelines | High |

## Context

These recommendations ensure comprehensive capture of both clinical and personal information essential for delivering integrated, person-centred care. The interventions support legal compliance while promoting individual autonomy and preferences in care delivery.
title: Health Interventions & Recommendations

content:
## Persona Overview

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | General Practitioner | Primary care physician responsible for overall health management, referrals, and coordination of care | Yes | Clinical assessment, care coordination, medication management, mental capacity assessment |
| CISV2.P2 | Social Worker | Professional supporting individuals with social care needs, safeguarding, and care planning | Yes | Safeguarding assessment, care planning, mental capacity assessment, advocacy |
| CISV2.P3 | Community Nurse | Nursing professional providing care in home and community settings | Yes | Clinical care delivery, medication administration, health monitoring, care coordination |
| CISV2.P4 | Care Coordinator | Professional responsible for coordinating multiple services and ensuring integrated care delivery | Yes | Service coordination, care planning, communication facilitation, documentation |
| CISV2.P5 | Hospital Clinician | Secondary care medical professional managing acute and specialist care | Yes | Specialist assessment, discharge planning, medication management, clinical documentation |
| CISV2.P6 | Care Home Manager | Professional managing residential care settings and resident wellbeing | No | Facility management, care oversight, regulatory compliance, staff coordination |
| CISV2.P7 | Allied Health Professional | Therapists and specialists providing targeted interventions (physiotherapy, occupational therapy, etc.) | Yes | Specialist assessment, therapy planning, functional improvement, equipment provision |

## Persona Details

**CISV2.P1 - General Practitioner**: Serves as the primary medical contact for individuals, maintaining comprehensive health records, prescribing medications, and coordinating referrals to specialist services. Requires full access to all clinical sections including problem lists, medications, investigations, and legal information for informed decision-making.

**CISV2.P2 - Social Worker**: Focuses on safeguarding, care planning, and supporting vulnerable individuals. Requires particular access to social context, safeguarding, legal information, and care planning sections to effectively advocate for and protect individuals.

**CISV2.P3 - Community Nurse**: Delivers hands-on care in community settings, requiring access to clinical information, care plans, medications, and individual requirements to provide safe and effective care during home visits.

**CISV2.P4 - Care Coordinator**: Acts as the central point for coordinating multiple services, requiring comprehensive access to all sections to ensure seamless service delivery and effective communication between providers.

**CISV2.P5 - Hospital Clinician**: Manages acute episodes and specialist care, requiring access to clinical history, current medications, allergies, and discharge planning information to ensure continuity of care.

**CISV2.P6 - Care Home Manager**: Oversees daily care provision in residential settings, requiring access to resident preferences, care plans, and health information to ensure appropriate support.

**CISV2.P7 - Allied Health Professional**: Provides specialised therapeutic interventions, requiring access to functional assessments, equipment needs, and therapy plans to deliver targeted support.
title: Generic Personas

content:
## Standards and Guidelines

1. Professional Record Standards Body (PRSB). Core Information Standard Implementation Guidance. London: PRSB; 2023.
2. NHS Digital. Dictionary of medicines and devices (dm+d) Implementation Guidance. Leeds: NHS Digital; 2023.
3. NHS Digital. SNOMED CT UK Edition Browser Guide. Leeds: NHS Digital; 2023.
4. NHS England. Accessible Information Standard (DCB1605). London: NHS England; 2016.

## Legislation and Regulation

5. UK Parliament. Equality Act 2010. London: The Stationery Office; 2010.
6. UK Parliament. Mental Capacity Act 2005. London: The Stationery Office; 2005.
7. UK Parliament. Mental Health Act 1983 (as amended 2007). London: The Stationery Office; 2007.
8. UK Parliament. Children Act 1989 and 2004. London: The Stationery Office; 2004.
9. UK Parliament. Care Act 2014. London: The Stationery Office; 2014.

## Clinical References

10. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE). Medicines Management Guidelines (NG5). London: NICE; 2015.
11. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE). Care and Support of Older People (QS13). London: NICE; 2011.
12. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE). End of Life Care Quality Standard (QS13). London: NICE; 2017.
13. NHS Data Dictionary. NHS Business Definitions and Data Standards. [https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/](https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/)

## Technical Standards

14. HL7 UK. UK Core FHIR R4 Implementation Guide. London: HL7 UK; 2023.
15. Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM). The UCUM Organization; 2023. [https://unitsofmeasure.org/](https://unitsofmeasure.org/)
16. DICOM Standards Committee. Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine. Rosslyn: NEMA; 2023.
title: References

Each process incorporates data collection checkpoints that build the comprehensive record while supporting care delivery.

content:
## Emergency Hospital Admission for Person with Complex Needs Mrs. Sarah Thompson, a 78-year-old with dementia, diabetes, and mobility impairments, is brought to the Emergency Department after a fall at home. The paramedics need immediate access to her critical information to ensure safe and appropriate care during this emergency admission. **Personas involved:** Hospital Clinician, Community Nurse, Care Coordinator **Linked processes:** CISV2.E, CISV2.D **Data elements involved:**

Element ID

Label

Used For

CISV2.DE15

NHS number

Patient identification and record retrieval

CISV2.DE197

Alerts

Immediate safety warnings (e.g., wandering risk, aggression triggers)

CISV2.DE1264

Allergies and adverse reactions

Medication safety checking for emergency treatment

CISV2.DE1313

Medications and medical devices

Current medication reconciliation to prevent interactions

CISV2.DE71

Reasonable adjustment

Ensuring appropriate communication methods due to dementia

CISV2.DE257

Mental capacity assessment

Determining consent pathways for emergency treatment

CISV2.DE465

Personal contacts

Contacting next of kin and primary carer

CISV2.DE452

Professional contacts

Notifying GP and care team of admission

CISV2.DE639

Problem list

Understanding existing conditions affecting treatment

CISV2.DE1506

Contingency/safety plans

Accessing pre-agreed crisis management strategies

## Coordinated Discharge Planning Mr. James Roberts, a 65-year-old recovering from a stroke, requires coordinated discharge planning from hospital to home with a comprehensive care package. Multiple professionals must collaborate to ensure safe transition and ongoing support. **Personas involved:** Hospital Clinician, Social Worker, Care Coordinator, Allied Health Professional **Linked processes:** CISV2.D, CISV2.C **Data elements involved:**

Element ID

Label

Used For

CISV2.DE556

Discharge details

Recording discharge destination and arrangements

CISV2.DE1432

Care and support plan

Developing post-discharge support arrangements

CISV2.DE1398

Equipment and adaptations

Arranging mobility aids and home modifications

CISV2.DE1001

Services and care

Setting up domiciliary care package

Professional contacts

Coordinating multidisciplinary team involvement

CISV2.DE149

Mobility

Assessing mobility needs for safe home environment

CISV2.DE1405

Plan and requested actions

Documenting rehabilitation goals and therapy schedules

CISV2.DE574

Future appointments

Scheduling follow-up clinics and therapy sessions

CISV2.DE1313

Medications and medical devices

Ensuring medication continuity and compliance aids

CISV2.DE953

Accommodation status

Verifying home suitability for discharge

## Initial Multi-Agency Assessment Emily Chen, a 42-year-old with learning disabilities and epilepsy, is referred for comprehensive assessment following concerns about her living situation and care needs. Multiple agencies must collaborate to develop an appropriate support plan. **Personas involved:** Social Worker, Community Nurse, Allied Health Professional, General Practitioner **Linked processes:** CISV2.B, CISV2.C **Data elements involved:**

Label

Used For

## Routine Care Review in Community Mr. David Patel, an 85-year-old living alone with mild cognitive impairment and multiple long-term conditions, receives his annual care review from the community nursing team to ensure his care plan remains appropriate. **Personas involved:** Community Nurse, General Practitioner, Care Coordinator **Linked processes:** CISV2.D, CISV2.B **Data elements involved:**

Label

Used For

## Crisis Intervention for Mental Health Tom Wilson, a 28-year-old with bipolar disorder, is experiencing a mental health crisis. His care coordinator activates his crisis plan while coordinating with emergency mental health services. **Personas involved:** Care Coordinator, Hospital Clinician, Social Worker **Linked processes:** CISV2.E, CISV2.D **Data elements involved:**

scenarios_metadata.title: Emergency Hospital Admission for Person with Complex Needs

title: User Scenarios

content:
## Decision-Support Logic

### Mental Capacity Assessment Decision

**Title:** Mental Capacity Assessment Requirement
**Description:** Determines when a formal mental capacity assessment should be initiated based on clinical indicators and care context.

| Condition | Value | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Cognitive impairment recorded (CISV2.DE126) | Present | Trigger capacity assessment |
| Safeguarding concern raised (CISV2.DE380) | TRUE | Trigger capacity assessment |
| Complex care decision required | YES | Trigger capacity assessment |
| Previous capacity assessment date (CISV2.DE258) | >12 months ago | Review assessment |
| Change in mental state | Significant | Trigger capacity assessment |
| All conditions | FALSE | No assessment required |

### Reasonable Adjustment Identification

**Title:** Reasonable Adjustment Requirements
**Description:** Identifies required adjustments based on person's impairments and individual requirements to ensure equitable care delivery.

| Condition | Value | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Visual impairment (CISV2.DE104) | Recorded | Provide large print/audio materials |
| Hearing impairment (CISV2.DE104) | Recorded | Arrange BSL interpreter/hearing loop |
| Learning disability (CISV2.DE104) | Present | Allow extra time, easy-read materials |
| Mobility needs (CISV2.DE154) | Wheelchair user | Ensure accessible venues/transport |
| Communication needs (CISV2.DE58) | Non-verbal | Implement agreed communication method |
| Cognitive impairment (CISV2.DE126) | Moderate-severe | Involve carer/advocate in appointments |

### Emergency Alert Activation

**Title:** Emergency Alert Response
**Description:** Determines immediate actions required when specific alerts are present during care encounters.

| Condition | Value | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Allergy severity (CISV2.DE1283) | Life-threatening | Display prominent warning, check all medications |
| Safeguarding indicator (CISV2.DE380) | TRUE | Notify safeguarding lead immediately |
| Violence/aggression risk (CISV2.DE1243) | HIGH | Implement safety protocol, 2-person visits |
| Absconding risk (CISV2.DE1243) | Present | Secure environment, notify security |
| DNACPR status (CISV2.DE1621) | Active | Display prominently, inform clinical team |
| Mental Health Act status (CISV2.DE332) | Current section | Check legal requirements for treatment |

### Discharge Safety Assessment

**Title:** Safe Discharge Decision
**Description:** Evaluates multiple factors to determine if discharge from hospital to community is safe and appropriate.

| Condition | Value | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Accommodation status (CISV2.DE959) | No fixed abode | Arrange temporary accommodation |
| Care package in place (CISV2.DE1003) | NO | Delay discharge, arrange services |
| Equipment required (CISV2.DE1400) | Not delivered | Delay discharge until equipment ready |
| Carer assessment completed | NO | Complete assessment before discharge |
| Medications reconciled (CISV2.DE1314) | NO | Complete reconciliation |
| Follow-up arranged (CISV2.DE576) | NO | Book required appointments |
| All conditions | SATISFIED | Approve discharge |

### Crisis Plan Activation

**Title:** Crisis Response Activation
**Description:** Determines when to activate crisis/contingency plans based on early warning signs and triggers.

| Condition | Value | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Relapse indicators present (CISV2.DE1534) | 2 or more | Activate crisis plan |
| Mental state deterioration | Rapid | Contact crisis team (CISV2.DE1518) |
| Medication non-compliance (CISV2.DE1374) | >7 days | Urgent medication review |
| Support network breakdown | Confirmed | Implement contingency supports |
| Self-harm ideation expressed | Active | Immediate risk assessment |
| Trigger factors present (CISV2.DE1507) | Multiple | Initiate preventive actions |
title: Decision-Support Logic

content:
## Indicators & Performance Metrics

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.01 | Reasonable Adjustments Recording Rate | Count of persons with completed reasonable adjustment assessments (CISV2.DE71) | Total persons with identified impairments (CISV2.DE100) | By impairment type, age group, ethnicity | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.02 | Mental Capacity Assessment Compliance | Count of persons with complex decisions having current MCA (CISV2.DE257 date <12 months) | Total persons requiring complex care decisions | By care setting, primary support reason | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.03 | Care Plan Review Timeliness | Count of care plans reviewed within planned timeframe (CISV2.DE1481) | Total active care plans (CISV2.DE1432) | By service type, complexity level | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.04 | Safeguarding Response Time | Count of safeguarding concerns addressed within 24 hours | Total safeguarding concerns raised (CISV2.DE380=TRUE) | By risk level, person demographics | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND.05 | Medication Reconciliation Completion | Count of admissions with completed medication reconciliation within 24 hours | Total hospital admissions (CISV2.DE528) | By admission type, ward | Daily |
| CISV2.IND.06 | Personal Preferences Documentation | Count of persons with completed 'About Me' sections (CISV2.DE52) | Total persons in service | By care setting, length of engagement | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.07 | Crisis Plan Effectiveness | Count of crisis episodes managed without hospital admission | Total crisis plan activations (CISV2.DE1506 used) | By diagnosis, previous admission history | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.08 | Allergy Alert Visibility | Count of clinical encounters with allergy status checked | Total clinical encounters (CISV2.DE508) | By encounter type, clinical setting | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND.09 | Discharge Planning Quality | Count of discharges with zero readmissions within 30 days | Total discharges (CISV2.DE558) | By discharge destination, care package type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.10 | Multi-Agency Coordination | Count of persons with 3+ professional contacts actively coordinating | Total persons with complex needs (multiple CISV2.DE639 entries) | By primary support reason, geographic area | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.11 | Equipment Provision Timeliness | Count of equipment requests fulfilled within agreed timeframe | Total equipment requests (CISV2.DE1400) | By equipment type, urgency level | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.12 | End of Life Preferences Recording | Count of persons 75+ with documented EoL preferences (CISV2.DE1615) | Total persons aged 75+ | By care setting, frailty score | Quarterly |
title: Indicators & Performance Metrics

content:
## Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR.01 | Real-time Alert Display | System must prominently display critical alerts (allergies, safeguarding, DNACPR) on all screens during care encounters | CISV2.E, CISV2.D | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.02 | Automated Capacity Check | System must prompt for mental capacity assessment when complex decisions are recorded without current assessment | CISV2.B, CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.03 | Multi-Agency Data Sharing | Enable secure sharing of core information between authorized professionals across organizational boundaries | CISV2.D | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.04 | Care Plan Versioning | Maintain full audit trail of care plan changes with ability to view previous versions and track modifications | CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.05 | Reasonable Adjustment Prompts | Automatically suggest reasonable adjustments based on recorded impairments when booking appointments | CISV2.A, CISV2.B | High |
| CISV2.FR.06 | Crisis Plan Activation | Enable one-click activation of crisis plans with automatic notification to designated contacts | CISV2.E | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.07 | Medication Reconciliation Workflow | Guide users through structured medication reconciliation process on admission/discharge/transfer | CISV2.D, CISV2.E | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.08 | Safeguarding Escalation | Automatically escalate safeguarding concerns to designated lead with acknowledgment tracking | CISV2.B, CISV2.E | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.09 | Discharge Checklist Validation | Enforce completion of safety checklist before allowing discharge documentation | CISV2.D | High |
| CISV2.FR.10 | Personal Narrative Integration | Allow persons/carers to directly input 'About Me' information through secure portal | CISV2.A | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.11 | Risk Assessment Aggregation | Aggregate risk indicators from multiple sources into unified risk profile with trend analysis | CISV2.B, CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.12 | Appointment Coordination | Automatically identify scheduling conflicts and coordinate multi-professional appointments | CISV2.D | Medium |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR.01 | Alert Response Time | Critical alerts must be displayed within 0.5 seconds of screen load | Performance | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.02 | Data Encryption | All personal data must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3+) | Security | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.03 | Accessibility Compliance | Interface must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards for accessibility | Usability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.04 | Audit Trail Completeness | System must log all data access, modifications, and sharing events with user attribution | Security/Compliance | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.05 | Mobile Responsiveness | Core functions must be fully operational on mobile devices for community staff | Usability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.06 | System Availability | 99.9% uptime for critical functions, 99.5% for non-critical functions | Reliability | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.07 | Concurrent User Support | Support minimum 10,000 concurrent users without performance degradation | Scalability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.08 | Data Retention Compliance | Automated retention policies aligned with NHS records management code | Compliance | High |
| CISV2.NFR.09 | Integration Standards | Support HL7 FHIR R4 for all clinical data exchange | Interoperability | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.10 | Backup Recovery Time | Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 2 hours, Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 15 minutes | Reliability | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.11 | Consent Management | Granular consent tracking with ability to restrict data sharing per person preferences | Compliance | High |
| CISV2.NFR.12 | Offline Capability | Critical read functions available offline with automatic sync when connected | Reliability | Medium |
title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
